CHARTS / BASKETS
% RETURN, REBASED · EOD 2026-07-07
DEFENSIVES LEADCyclicals -2.2% vs defensives on the month
Rotation ReadCitrini · claude

The rotation is a violent pull out of mega-cap tech and speculative software into healthcare, utilities, and banks. XLV leads sectors at +8.58% one-month and XLU follows at +7.22%, while XLK dumps -6.32% and XLC crashes -6.48% over the same window.

The Mag 7 basket is down -9.6% in a month with MSFT the laggard, and software is getting routed at -16.7% with TEAM in the bottom. You are watching capital desert the AI frenzy and rotate into the most defensive corners of the market.

The broadening thesis is real but it is broadening into value and defensives, not into high-beta risk. XLI is up +6.0% one-month with PH leading and industrials as a theme up +23.0% YTD, so cyclical strength coexists with the defensive bid.

Banks at +9.0% one-month led by USB confirm you are getting a real rotation into financials and industrials alongside the healthcare and utility rally. The central tension is whether this is a healthy expansion of leadership or the start of a risk-off correction disguised as rotation.

The stretched ratios tell you it is the latter. MTUM/SPY sits at a z-score of 3.12, in the top 2% of its yearly range, and IWM/SPY is at 2.40 in the top 1%, which means small-caps and momentum ex-tech are both extended.

VLUE/SPY at 2.39 and XLI/XLP at 2.16 confirm the value and cyclical trade is crowded. The trades working are GLP-1 at +13.8% one-month led by VKTX and biotech at +11.8% led by MRNA, both healthcare-adjacent and both consistent with a defensive tilt.

Semis are still up +95.8% YTD but only +7.2% in the last month with SMCI as the laggard, and AI infra is down -7.0% one-month despite being up +102.8% YTD. The YTD leaders are rolling over and the laggards are catching a bid.

Energy at -5.9% one-month with OXY lagging despite XLE up +18.7% YTD shows you the commodity tail is fading. Gold miners at -10.1% and uranium at -15.0% confirm the real-asset trade is unwinding.

The flip point is XLK/SPY, still at a z-score of 2.35 in the top 7% of its range with +17.6% YTD outperformance. If that ratio breaks below its current level and tech fails to reclaim leadership, you have a correction.

If it holds and tech stabilizes, this rotation is a healthy pause.

Capital is fleeing mega-cap tech for defensives and cyclicals simultaneously, and the breadth push is stretched enough to signal a correction risk.

BULL

Industrials at +23.0% YTD, banks at +9.0% one-month, and IWM/SPY at 2.40 signal a genuine broadening into value and small-caps that extends the bull market beyond the Mag 7. VLUE/SPY at 2.39 with +32.7% YTD confirms real commitment to the rotation.

BEAR

Mag 7 at -9.6%, software at -16.7%, and XLC at -6.48% one-month with XLC/SPY at -2.89 in the bottom 2% of its range tell you the leadership group is breaking. Defensive leadership from XLV and XLU alongside the SaaS and quantum collapse at -16.4% is a risk-off pattern.

TELL

XLK/SPY at 2.35 in the top 7% of its range. If this ratio holds and XLK stabilizes, the rotation is healthy. If it breaks down through the current z-score, tech capitulation accelerates and the defensive bid becomes a full flight to safety.

⚠ IWM/SPY at **2.40** in the top **1%** and VLUE/SPY at **2.39** in the top **1%** signal a healthy broadening into small-caps and value that extends the bull market. vs Mag 7 at **-9.6%**, software at **-16.7%**, and SaaS at **-16.1%** one-month with XLC/SPY at **-2.89** in the bottom **2%** signal the leadership group is breaking down in a way that usually precedes a broader correction.: Both can be true early in a rotation, but when the new leadership is this stretched on the same timeframe the old leadership is collapsing, the broadening usually fails and everything rolls over together. Trust the bear read until XLK/SPY at **2.35** stabilizes.
⚠ XLI up **+6.0%** one-month and XLI/XLP at **2.16** in the top **1%** with industrials up **+23.0%** YTD signal a pro-cyclical risk-on rotation. vs XLV leading at **+8.58%** and XLU at **+7.22%** one-month alongside gold miners at **-10.1%** and uranium at **-15.0%** signal a defensive haven bid and commodity weakness inconsistent with a growth acceleration.: Cyclicals and defensives leading simultaneously is a late-cycle signature. The defensive outperformance of XLV and XLU over XLI on a one-month basis says capital prefers safety over growth exposure. Trust the defensive read, and watch XLI/XLP at **2.16** for the cyclical bid to crack.

The most extreme reversion candidate is MTUM/SPY at 3.12, in the top 2% of its yearly range with +20.4% YTD. Momentum ex-tech is this stretched and the Mag 7 is down -9.6% in a month, which tells you the rotation trade is crowded and ripe for a snapback.

IWM/SPY at 2.40 in the top 1% and VLUE/SPY at 2.39 in the top 1% are the same story: everyone piled into small-cap value at once. XLI/XLP at 2.16 in the top 1% with +6.1% YTD is your cyclical-vs-defensive ratio at its limit, and XRT/XLY at 2.12 in the top 1% confirms the consumer-discretionary-to-retail spread is maxed.

These are all mean-reversion candidates if the cyclical bid falters. On the confirmed-trend side, XLC/SPY at -2.89 in the bottom 2% with -14.4% YTD is a structural breakdown, not a reversion setup.

XLY/SPY at -2.09 in the bottom 5% with -9.6% YTD is the same: consumer discretionary relative strength is broken and you do not catch that. MCHI/SPY at -2.69 in the bottom 1% with -24.5% YTD and FXI/EEM at -2.30 in the bottom 2% with -33.1% YTD confirm China exposure is a structural loser.

SOXX/SMH at 2.62 in the top 2% with +14.9% YTD is the one ratio that looks like a genuine structural shift, where the US-listed semiconductor ETF is outperforming the VanEck version by enough to signal a real preference for domestic chip exposure. SPHB/SPY at 2.47 in the top 2% with +17.5% YTD confirms high-beta ex-tech is the crowded trade.

XBI/XLV at 2.05 in the top 1% with +24.4% YTD is biotech outperforming healthcare pharma, consistent with MRNA leading the biotech basket at +11.8% one-month. That one is trend, not reversion.
